channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Former Syrian prison official charged in US with torture
item.description: A former Syrian official under the rule of former President Bashar Assad was federally indicted in the U.S. for torturing prisoners held at Damascus Central Prison from 2005-08, according to a Thursday release from the Justice Department. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Thousands of Syrians celebrate in central Damascus during first Friday prayers since Assad's fall
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Thousands of Syrians gathered Friday in Damascus' historic main mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since the ouster of President Bashar Assad, while giant crowds celebrated in the capital's largest square.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France's Macron names centrist ally Bayrou as next prime minister
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron named centrist ally François Bayrou as prime minister, after a historic parliamentary vote ousted the previous government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: Saudi Arabia says that to host the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, it will build or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports and public transport in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John And Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EU nations agree a crackdown on migrant smugglers. Some fear it may target people trying to help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union countries agreed on Friday a draft law aimed at preventing and countering migrant smuggling, which critics say could be used to target people or charity groups that try to help migrants in trouble.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bringing hope and joy: superheroes for children with cancer in Kosovo
item.description: As the year draws to a close, a remarkable initiative involving “superheroes” clad in unique costumes brings unexpected joy — not only to the sick children but also, perhaps even more deeply, to their parents.
item.dc:creator: Florent Bajrami And Llazar Semini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Traffic blockades held throughout Serbia against populist government over roof collapse tragedy
item.description: Residents throughout Serbia have stopped in silence for 15 minutes as part of persistent protests against the populist leadership following the collapse last month of a concrete canopy in the country’s north that killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Jovana Gec, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: President Macron names centrist ally Bayrou as France's next prime minister
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has named centrist ally François Bayrou as prime minister, after a historic parliamentary vote ousted the previous government last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia targets Ukrainian infrastructure with a massive attack by cruise missiles and drones
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a massive aerial attack against Ukraine on Friday, firing 93 cruise and ballistic missiles and almost 200 drones, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, describing it as one of the heaviest bombardments of the country's energy sector since Russia's full-sc
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria’s rebels face a new battle: Winning over the West
item.description: The emerging transitional government in Syria faces a daunting road ahead as it looks to unite a vast coalition of rebel groups and appeal to Western nations concerned about whether the country will suppress minority communities or become a haven for violent extremists.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew barred from UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew has been barred from the U.K. because of concerns he poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Blinken urges Mideast nations to support a peaceful Syrian political transition
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken is pressing ahead with efforts to unify Middle East nations in supporting a peaceful political transition in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian forces edge closer to a key eastern Ukraine city in intense fighting, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Fighting around the key eastern Ukraine city of Pokrovsk is “extremely intense” after a monthslong Russian push, Ukraine’s top military commander said, with analysts estimating Russian forces are now within just a few kilometers (miles) of the city.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indian teen Gukesh Dommaraju becomes the youngest chess world champion after beating Chinese rival
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Indian teenager Gukesh Dommaraju became the youngest chess world champion Thursday after beating the defending champion Ding Liren of China in the final match of their series in Singapore.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands attend the funeral of a Taliban minister killed by an Islamic State suicide bomber
item.description: GARDA SERAI, Afghanistan (AP) — Thousands of people attended the funeral on Thursday of a Taliban minister killed in a suicide bombing in Afghanistan's capital, Kabul, the day before that was claimed by the Islamic State group.
item.dc:creator: SAIFULLAH ZAHIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 110 people have died in 7 weeks of post-election protests in Mozambique, rights groups say
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — More than 100 people including children have been killed by security forces in post-election protests in Mozambique that have lasted nearly two months, rights and civil society groups said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers call for intelligence assessment on ending U.S. support for Ukraine
item.description: Lawmakers want the key intelligence chiefs to assess U.S. security risks if America stops providing weapons to Ukraine and Russian wins the war. The lawmakers are pressing for a provision to be added to the National Defense Authorization Act, which the House is expected to vote on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Senators call on Biden to brief upper chamber on efforts to return Austin Tice from Syria
item.description: In a bipartisan push, senators have called on President Biden to brief the upper chamber to provide specific details on efforts underway to secure the release of Austin Tice from captivity in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Navy defeats Houthi attacks in Gulf of Aden for second time in weeks
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale (DDG 106) and USS O’Kane (DDG 77) successfully defeated a range of Houthi-launched weapons while transiting the Gulf of Aden for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China’s sci-fi spherical Death Star-like robot cop uses AI, facial recognition to track criminals
item.description: Chinese robotics company, Logon Technology, unveiled the RT-G autonomous spherical robot which will use AI and facial recognition to aid law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilian President Lula in ICU after surgery to drain head hemorrhage, hospital says
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is recovering in an ICU after undergoing surgery to drain an intracranial hemorrhage following a fall at home in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Journalists' rights group says 104 media workers killed this year
item.description: More than half of the journalists and media workers killed perished during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese military makes massive deployment around Taiwan to send 'very simple' message
item.description: Taiwan is raising concerns about a massive deployment of Chinese naval ships and military planes in the past 24 hours, saying the build-up could eventually lead to war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden seems to take credit for Assad's downfall amid fears of Islamic State revival
item.description: President Biden angered some critics when he seemed to claim his administration's Mideast policies led to the downfall of Syria's Bashar Assad on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire in 2019 nearly destroyed Paris' beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral reopens its doors for first time since devastating 2019 fire
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, WFLA, NBC
